g.text: 2 hex chars per byte × 32 bytes = 64 chars

text.tspan: /* Correct: prints all 8 hex digits, zero-padded */
/* WRONG: %x omits leading zeros — "ba7816bf" OK but
H[7]=0x00... would print too few chars */

g.text: printf("%x", H[i]); /* BAD for zero-padded words */

text.tspan: /* Correct: prints all 8 hex digits, zero-padded */
/* WRONG: %x omits leading zeros — "ba7816bf" OK but
H[7]=0x00... would print too few chars */

g.text: Step 2: extract bytes MSB-first | uint32_t → 4 bytes
Step 3: encode each byte | byte → 2 hex chars
Step 4: concatenate | 8 words x 8 chars = 64 chars
